title: Why it’s a dangerous time to brush your teeth with toothpaste

chunks.title: Fluoride and Toothpaste Concerns
chunks.summary: The discussion opens with concerns about fluoride in toothpaste and the presence of microplastics. The speaker emphasizes that they do not use toothpaste and explains their reasons, citing potential harm from excess fluoride and microplastics in dental products.

chunks.title: Nutrient Deficiencies in Vegetarian and Vegan Diets
chunks.summary: The speaker talks about the nutrient deficiencies often seen in vegetarian and vegan diets, particularly in relation to vitamin K2 and other fat-soluble vitamins. They argue that these nutrients, crucial for dental health, are primarily found in animal foods and are important for preventing cavities.
chunks.transcript: fluoride and a lot of the water has fluoride so there's fluoride everywhere but the reason we're using so much fluoride is because people are massively nutrient deficient kind of back to the same this is why it's so dangerous to be a vegetarian or vegan because even without looking at soil depletion of nutrients which we'll get into a vegetarian or vegan is likely going to be deficient in vitamin k2 and other fat soluble vitamins so k2 is menoquinone you've probably talked about this on your podcast but k2 is so freaking critical um for tooth health along with d and e and other fat soluble vitamins which are predominantly found in animal foods you can't get much of them if any in plant foods some people would say oh natto is fermented soybeans but the vitamin k2 and natto i believe is mostly mk7 not mk4 and i could be wrong but it's one of those two isoforms of metaquinone and mk4 is the one you really want and it's made by bacteria it's not made by the soybeans it's made by a fermentation process in the bacteria so soil depletion of nutrients yes but vegetarians and vegans definitely nutrient deficient leading to

chunks.title: Cavities and Dental Health
chunks.summary: This segment focuses on the causes of cavities, suggesting that they are not solely the result of sugar consumption, but also related to nutrient deficiencies. The speaker details their own dental health regime and claims that a diet rich in animal fats has prevented their dental issues.
chunks.transcript: cavities cavities are not exclusively the consumption of sugar processed sugar isn't a good thing to put in your mouth you know that i'm not afraid of putting fruit in my mouth or honey in my mouth i'm a very healthy dentition i don't have any cavities i get a lot of honey every day but i brush my teeth once a day with no toothpaste and i don't need fluoride because i have tons of fat soluble nutrients from animals in my diet and that's another reason

chunks.title: Microplastics in Fish and Other Diets
chunks.summary: The dangers of consuming microplastics through fish and the risks associated with various diets, including veganism, vegetarianism, and pescetarianism, are discussed. The speaker highlights the complications brought by industrial pollutants in modern diets.
chunks.transcript: that i don't use toothpaste is this idea of microplastics like i don't want anything extra in my toothpaste whether it's peg polyethylene glycol or these supposed microplastics like polyoximer 404 and there's all kinds of crappy microplastics in toothpaste some toothpaste has carrageenan which is a an algal derivative it's like a long chain molecule from algae that's a thickener that has been shown to be damaging in the human gut so why would you want more chemicals in your mouth in your diet that's just a horrible thing for humans so dangerous time to be a vegetarian or a vegan dangerous time to be a pescetarian because of microplastics and the fish we can talk about that dangerous time to

chunks.title: Hunter-Gatherer Dental Health
chunks.summary: The speaker compares the dental health of modern humans to that of hunter-gatherers like the Maasai and Hadza. They note that traditional diets are associated with better dental health and attribute poor dental health in some tribes to factors such as sugar and tobacco consumption.
chunks.transcript: brush your teeth with toothpaste how about that yeah well i mean here's the thing like when you visited the tribes now i have seen some tribesmen where their dental tea their dental health looks terrible now are those the ones that had like corn and sugar and stuff brought in from the missionaries so there's differences so if you look at the messiah the maasai generally have big broad white smiles and the hadza are hunter gatherers and a lot of the hadza have brown stains on their teeth but the hadza are well known to be very addicted to tobacco and marijuana and they also probably have exposure to large amounts of fluoride in the water source so you're back to fluoride so i don't know if the maasai don't have exposure to these fluoride water sources but even in tanzania in the in the cities you'll see people who have grown up in the cities and they have these big brown stains on their teeth from fluorosis from excess fluoride in the water when they're growing up and this is massive amounts of fluoride in the water in tanzania so you get these big brown stains on the teeth but the hots have a lot of those brown stains and it's hard to know whether that's smoking marijuana fluoride in the water you know where they getting it from are they getting a contaminated river but i don't think that the hots necessarily have cavities as much as they have a lot of brown stains people will look at them and be like their teeth don't look great but if you look at generally hunter-gatherers like the maasai if they don't smoke a lot they have big broad white smiles i mean there's tons of examples of this weston price's book nutrition and physical degeneration is the best illustration of big broad wide jaws and the hypothesis would be that adequate fat soluble nutrition as a child in utero even into adulthood helps us get broad

chunks.title: Impact of Childhood Nutrition on Dental Structure
chunks.summary: This segment discusses how childhood nutrition affects dental structure and jaw formation. The speaker posits that insufficient intake of fat-soluble vitamins during crucial developmental stages can lead to misformed jaws and respiratory issues.
